global_layoffs.description: Planning sweeping layoffs affecting 20% of 79,000 workforce to offset AI infrastructure spending. CEO Zuckerberg says projects that used to require big teams now accomplished by a single person.
Confirmed 16,000 job cuts, nearly 10% of workforce, flattening management layers and bolstering AI infrastructure.
CEO Dorsey cut company from 10,000 to under 6,000. First S&P 500 company to explicitly halve workforce for AI: 'Intelligence tools have changed what it means to build a company.'
AI-driven restructuring of logistics platforms. Executives stated traditional approaches to writing code are becoming obsolete.
Restructuring across European operations as AI reshapes telecom equipment sector.
Workforce reduction at semiconductor equipment leader amid industry restructuring.
1,500 Reality Labs jobs cut as Meta pivots from metaverse to AI research. CTO Bosworth calls it initiative to simplify operations.
CEO stated 'AI is changing the world' forcing 6% global workforce reduction across both platforms.
Singapore home design platform cut 1,000 jobs to accelerate AI adoption across its digital interior-design marketplace.
Online grocery company cutting 1,000 roles from 20,000 workforce as AI reshapes fulfillment operations.
800 jobs cut as company shifts to AI-powered listings and automated seller tools.
Cut 15% of workforce to prioritize AI-focused teams and products while streamlining sales structure.
Workforce reduction as AI automates home services matching and customer interactions.
Job cuts as company restructures around cloud AI services.
Latin America's largest e-commerce company cuts roles as AI automates logistics and seller operations.

Largest layoff in Oracle's 48-year history. 20,000–30,000 employees terminated via email with no notice. Cuts fund $156B AI infrastructure buildout. TD Cowen: expected to free $8–10B cash flow. AI coding tools cited by co-CEO Sicilia: 'smaller engineering teams deliver more complete solutions'.
10% of global workforce. CEO Cannon-Brookes: cuts needed to 'self-fund AI investment and enterprise sales in the AI era'.
Reality Labs, social media and recruiting teams affected. Part of ongoing AI-driven efficiency push alongside $135B AI capex planned for 2026.
Post-VMware integration cuts in sales and support. Company pivoting resources toward AI networking portfolio.
Deus Ex studio restructuring. Part of broader gaming industry consolidation amid AI content generation pressure.

gulf_mena_automation.description: Saudi Arabia officially designates 2026 as the Year of Artificial Intelligence. Cabinet decision under MBS as SDAIA chair.
Applied AI Bootcamp launched in Riyadh providing intensive hands-on AI and data science training for Saudi nationals.
World's largest government data center (480MW, 30M+ sqft, Tier IV) under construction in Riyadh. $2.7B contract awarded to Albawani.
Microsoft commits to helping 3 million people in Saudi Arabia acquire AI skills by 2030.
PIF finalizing revised 2026-2030 strategy with ~15% capex reduction but accelerating AI infrastructure investment through HUMAIN.
Saudi student Al-Jawhara Al-Qahtani wins global Grand Prize at Geneva Inventions Exhibition for AI medical platform PulMind.
Developing advanced data centers in KSA through partnerships with global companies for AI technology localization.
5th investment package worth SAR 11B awarded at MIPIM 2026. Total private sector investment in the park now exceeds SAR 20B.

saudi_policy_updates.title: 2026 Designated Year of AI
saudi_policy_updates.description: Saudi Cabinet officially designates 2026 as Year of Artificial Intelligence. Policy umbrella for new incentives, regulatory fast-tracks, and AI procurement preferences across government.

saudi_policy_updates.title: AI Sector Growth: $9.1B Funding
saudi_policy_updates.description: 664 companies now operating in Saudi data and AI space. Government AI spending up 56.25% in 2024 vs 2023. $9.1B secured across 70 investment deals.

saudi_policy_updates.title: Shaheen III Supercomputer & Hexagon
saudi_policy_updates.description: Launch of Shaheen III supercomputer and 480MW Hexagon Data Center. Kingdom targeting 3-6 GW of AI computing capacity.

saudi_policy_updates.title: GPAI Membership & ICAIRE Center
saudi_policy_updates.description: Saudi Arabia joins Global Partnership on AI and hosts UNESCO's International Center for AI Research and Ethics in Riyadh.

saudi_policy_updates.title: PIF Strategy Recalibration 2026-2030
saudi_policy_updates.description: PIF finalizing revised strategy with ~15% capex reduction. Construction contract awards fell 60% to below $30B. AI infrastructure is fastest-growing segment.

ai_workforce_signals.headline: 20% of 2026 Tech Layoffs Explicitly AI-Driven
ai_workforce_signals.description: RationalFX analysis shows 9,238 of 45,363 tech layoffs in 2026 are directly linked to AI and automation. Block, WiseTech Global, and Livspace lead the AI-attributed cuts.

ai_workforce_signals.headline: Meta Planning 20% Workforce Cut (~15,800 Jobs)
ai_workforce_signals.description: Reuters reports Meta planning sweeping layoffs to offset $600B AI data center investment while preparing for AI-assisted worker efficiency. Would be largest cuts since 2022-2023.

ai_workforce_signals.headline: Young Workers Hit Hardest by AI Displacement
ai_workforce_signals.description: Anthropic research finds job-finding rates for workers aged 22-25 in AI-exposed occupations fell 14% since ChatGPT launch. US workers in 20s in AI-exposed jobs saw unemployment rise 3%.

ai_workforce_signals.headline: Saudi Arabia Ranks 14th Global AI Index, 1st in Arab World
ai_workforce_signals.description: Kingdom ranks 14th globally in 2025 AI Index, 3rd in OECD AI Policy Observatory, 1st in Arab world for AI model development per Stanford indicators. Over 11,000 specialists trained.

ai_workforce_signals.headline: Microsoft to Train 3 Million Saudis in AI by 2030
ai_workforce_signals.description: Microsoft commits to AI skills training for 3 million people in Saudi Arabia, part of broader EMEA initiative aligned with Year of AI designation.

ai_workforce_signals.headline: Morgan Stanley: AI Could Eliminate 200,000 European Banking Jobs
ai_workforce_signals.description: Morgan Stanley report warns European banks may cut 200,000 roles via AI automation over 5 years, freeing €15B in cost savings. Gulf banking sector watches closely: GCC banks, including Saudi lenders, face comparable efficiency pressure as SAMA accelerates fintech licensing.

weekly_stats.notable_trend: Q1 2026 closes with 79,000+ tech layoffs, led by Oracle's 30,000-job cut — largest in its 48-year history. AI explicitly cited in 54% of cuts. Atlassian (1,600), Broadcom (1,200) and Meta (700) add to the wave. Morgan Stanley warns 200,000 European banking jobs at risk from AI.
